No appearance by moving party or attorney for plaintiff David Calkins Esq.

Both moving defendants confirm that plaintiff has been served the moving papers and other pleadings previously served. The attorney for the moving party has contacted attorney David Calkins by both email and phone to advise his appearance at the hearing is required.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification.

Defendants, The Regents' Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Failure To Respond To Discovery Pursuant To Court Order is GRANTED. The motion is unopposed. The showing before the Court includes violation of a Discovery Order which supports imposition of terminating sanctions. It is possible plaintiff Ethel Barajas has abandoned the lawsuit. On September 29, 2020 attorney Calkins dismissed the Pedro Barajas action. Service of pleadings has thereafter been in digital format. Plaintiff has not responded to motions or discovery pleadings since September 29.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling and that gives a detailed recitation of non-compliance with discovery production orders. The [proposed] order must be emailed in Word (.doc) format to the judge pro tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com.
